title: A2UI × Astryx — Gap Analysis & Feature Priorities

blocks.title: A2UI × Astryx
blocks.subtitle: Where Meta's interaction kit is deep and ours is thin — 26 genuine gaps, folded into ~18 A2UI-native atoms across 3 waves.

blocks.title: The finding
items: Astryx ships 92 components; A2UI ships 498 — we're 5× larger, not behind on breadth.
Of Astryx's 92, ~66 already have A2UI equivalents.
The real gap is a specific band: stateful INPUT + app-frame chrome.
26 genuine gaps collapse to ~18 atoms by folding variants into mode/flags — not 1:1 clones.

blocks.caption: Astryx category → what A2UI is missing
rows: Field, Typeahead/Power Search, Tokenizer, Date/Time, Number, File, Calendar
Toolbar, More Menu, Toggle Button(+group), Icon Button, Button Group
App Shell, Aspect Ratio, Resize Handle

blocks.title: Wave 1 — Input primitives (highest leverage)
blocks.description: form_field wrapper first, then datetime_input (mode: range/datetime/time), number_input, tag_input, search_input, file_input. Closes the structural gap. 9 Astryx atoms → 6 A2UI.

blocks.title: Wave 2 — App chrome
blocks.description: app_shell, side_nav, toolbar, more_menu, popover. Turns the catalogue from 'renders content' into 'frames an app'.

blocks.title: Wave 3 — Polish & a11y
blocks.description: mega_menu, lightbox, aspect_ratio, resize_handle, visually_hidden, timestamp, token.

blocks.title: Consolidation discipline
blocks.text: Fold Astryx's fragmented variants into mode/flags — same counterweight principle as the chat_thread router. selectable_card / clickable_card become flags on info_card, not new atoms.
Not a copy of Astryx — the A2UI-native equivalent, kept flat where their catalogue fragments.
